text:
Once upon a time, in a sleepy town bathed in moonlight, there lived a clumsy owl named Oliver. Famous far and wide for dropping his feathers whenever wings caught wind. His favorite thing to drool over were old tales.

One foggy morning, while spying through the windows of Widow Cordelia's stone cottage, he heard tales of a magical forest deeper than anyone's. Creatures so wonderful they made dreams seem real. Oliver felt his wings ache with curiosity. Maybe, he thought, they could glide there.

He plotted. Each night saw him flapping down, peeking. He learned secrets of shy hawks and wary deer. One particularly clear night, after a snack of crunchy leaves, he soared.

Then came trouble. His wings sang. Ice. Everywhere.

He hovered, looking dumb. Blades. Nothing.

Back at home, his keeper said, "Don't you dare fly away again."

But Oliver did.

Into dreams. Leaves changed into stars. He saw mountains that spoke
